name: Opengist
short_desc: Self-hosted pastebin powered by Git — a personal GitHub Gist.
author: Thomas Miceli
description: Opengist is a self-hosted pastebin powered by Git, a lightweight alternative to GitHub Gist. Create, share, and version code snippets; every gist is a Git repository you can clone, pull, and push. Features include syntax highlighting, Markdown and CSV rendering, embeddable gists, OAuth login (GitHub/GitLab/Gitea/OIDC), and full-text search.

form_fields.label: Session secret key
form_fields.hint: Auto-generated secret used for the session store and to encrypt MFA data. Leave as generated — pinning it keeps you logged in across restarts/updates.

form_fields.label: Public URL (for internet access)
form_fields.hint: Leave BLANK for LAN access (auto-filled from your host IP and chosen port). To expose Opengist over the internet — and for OAuth logins, embeds, and clone URLs to be correct — set this to the public URL that routes to the app.
